title: Dynamical Decoupling vs. Baseline Under an Idle Window
protocol: Field 27 (LOCK -> SPAM gate -> principal job -> analyze -> verdict)
author: Derek Hone (Remnant Fieldworks Inc.)

qubits.note: Same series-consistent pair as ARK-447; lowest connected-pair readout-error sum.

spam_gate.SPAM_P_role: gating diagnostic only (|+> ~0.5 expected); NEVER subtracted from DENY leakage

principal_job.reason: SPAM gate failed; per LOCKED preregistration the principal job is only executed after the SPAM gate passes. Execution halted.

verdict: NO VERDICT — experiment aborted at the SPAM gate before any DD-vs-baseline data was obtained.

gate_stop_explanation.interpretation: The payload qubit's |+> readout symmetry drifted 0.022 from the ideal 0.5, exceeding the preregistered +/-0.02 tolerance by 0.002 (~2 sigma at 2048 shots). This is consistent with a marginal statistical fluctuation and/or minor readout drift on the calibration snapshot used. The LOCKED preregistration does NOT permit re-running the gate, loosening the threshold, or proceeding on a failed gate. Execution was halted honestly.
gate_stop_explanation.no_fabrication_statement: No principal-job data exists for ARK-448. No DD-vs-baseline result is claimed. This record documents a preregistered gate abort, not a scientific result about dynamical decoupling.

budget_note: IBM open-plan budget was conserved: the principal job was never submitted, preserving remaining seconds for future runs.
